title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa describes how to build and scale AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns including core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches used in their OG Assist product.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov walks through how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent operating across thousands of government workflows in production. The talk covers concrete architecture patterns: the agent core loop, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches, with attention to evals, long-context handling, and the A2A protocol. The focus is on the engineering practices needed to run agents reliably at scale rather than prototype demonstrations.
